concerns.text: FR-007 ('System MUST run on CPU-only hardware...') anchors to US-3, but the constraint is a global infrastructure requirement affecting US-1 (download) and US-2 (entropy computation) as well. While anchored, the scope is broader than US-3 alone. Consider re-anchoring to a global constraint or splitting to ensure full coverage of the pipeline's hardware dependency.

FR-008 (Sensitivity analysis on parameter r) anchors to US-3. However, the Edge Cases section and US-2 imply the need for robustness in the entropy computation itself. While anchored to US-3 (reporting), the requirement to 'sweep' implies a computational loop that might be better scoped in US-2 or explicitly noted as a post-processing step in US-3. The anchor exists, but the functional split between 'compute' (US-2) and 'sweep/report' (US-3) is slightly ambiguous.

SC-006 (Statistical power) anchors to US-3. The user story US-3 focuses on fitting the model and generating the report. While power analysis is part of the scientific rigor, it is not explicitly described as a deliverable or step within US-3's 'Acceptance Scenarios'. Ensure the implementation plan for US-3 explicitly includes the generation of this post-hoc power calculation to fully satisfy the success criterion.
